cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find the number of positive integers n ≤ 500 such that when the sum of cubes from 1 to n is divided by n+1, the remainder is n. Let me try to figure this out step by step. First, let me recall the formula for the sum of cubes. I remember that the sum of the first n cubes is given by the square of the sum of the first n natural numbers. So: $$ \sum_{k=1}^{n} k^3 = \left( \frac{n(n+1)}{2} \right)^2 $$ So the problem states that when this sum is divided by n+1, the remainder is n. In mathematical terms, this means: $$ \left( \frac{n(n+1)}{2} \right)^2 \equiv n \pmod{n+1} $$ Hmm, okay. Let me try to simplify this congruence. Maybe I can manipulate the left-hand side first.
